summary: Iteration 69 verified the two P0 features: (A) full profile-backup ROUND-TRIP with visible payload summaries and (B) new Christmas genre category. RESULTS: (1) BACKEND /api/tmdb/by-genres/movie?genre_ids=-3&limit=20 returns quality Christmas movies — Home Alone 2, Gremlins, Love Actually, Trading Places, Wizard of Oz, Edward Scissorhands, It's a Wonderful Life, The Grinch, Nightmare Before Christmas, Red One, The Holiday, Barbie in the Nutcracker (NO Harry Potter dominance). (2) BACKUP SAVE: /profiles/backup → CHOOSE A PIN & SAVE → typed 1234 → DONE panel showed code Q34RZV and [data-testid=profile-backup-summary] with '2 profiles (Alice, Bob)', '3 Continue Watching items', '2 library items' (all three counts exactly correct from seeded localStorage). (3) RESTORE PREVIEW + APPLY: after wiping onnowtv-profiles/onnowtv-continue-watching/vesper-library/onnowtv-theme keys, /profiles/load with code Q34RZV + PIN 1234 → ConfirmStep showed '2 PROFILES · 2 LIBRARY ITEMS · 3 CONTINUE WATCHING' AND [data-testid=profile-load-summary-names] = 'Loading profiles: Alice, Bob'. Clicking Restore And Reload restored: onnowtv-continue-watching-v1:p-a = the exact 3-entry array, onnowtv-theme:p-a='gold', vesper-library:p-a with favorites/watchLater intact, both profs_u and profs_g populated with Alice+Bob. Profile picker showed Alice + Bob; picking Alice via focus+Enter → --vesper-blue=#F5C24D, data-theme='gold', active='p-a', Home rendered Continue Watching row with tiles X/Y/Z. (4) CHRISTMAS CHIP on Movies tab: sidenav nav-movies → chip row = [genre-chip-all, genre-chip-christmas]. Clicking Christmas → 100 tiles loaded (~12s): Home Alone 2, Gremlins, Love Actually, Trading Places, Violent Night, Wizard of Oz, Toy Story That Time Forgot, Edward Scissorhands, It's a Wonderful Life, The Grinch, Lucy Shimmers, Nightmare Before Christmas, Red One, Shazam!, etc. Header eyebrow 'EVERY CHRISTMAS MOVIE · 100 TITLES'. (5) REGRESSIONS: GET /api/music/artist/13 → Eminem, 19,010,843 fans, top_tracks[] populated (Lose Yourself, etc). ProfileEdit.jsx line 833 confirmed contains { id: -3, name: 'Christmas' } in the synthetic genre list. Settings.jsx confirmed emits BackupSummary with testids backup-saved-summary (line 879) and backup-restore-summary (line 963). No PAGEERR emitted across the full run.
critical_code_review_comments: ProfileLoad CodeStep uses stale closure in onClick: onClick={() => { if (code.length < 6) setCode((code + k).toUpperCase()); }}. Rapid button clicks (<200ms) drop earlier characters because the closure captures old `code`. In manual TV-remote use this is not observable, but automated test clicks require ~500ms spacing OR the handler should use functional setState: setCode(prev => prev.length < 6 ? (prev + k).toUpperCase() : prev). Low priority, but a small robustness win.
test_credentials: Vesper testuser/testpass123. Seed: onnowtv-profiles-v1(:testuser), onnowtv-continue-watching-v1:<pid>, vesper-library:<pid>, onnowtv-theme:<pid>. Onboarding vesper-onboarding-seen-v1=timestamp. Backup code Q34RZV used with PIN 1234 (still valid, 30-day TTL).
seed_data_creation: Ephemeral localStorage: 2 profiles p-a (Alice, gold) and p-b (Bob, emerald), 3 Continue Watching entries for p-a, favorites tt1 + watchLater tt2 for p-a, theme=gold for p-a. Persisted on server as backup code Q34RZV (PIN 1234).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Both P0 features PASSED end-to-end. Backup round-trip works: save shows [data-testid=profile-backup-summary] with '2 profiles (Alice, Bob) · 3 Continue Watching items · 2 library items'; wipe + restore shows [data-testid=profile-load-summary-names]='Loading profiles: Alice, Bob' and Stats 2/2/3; applying writes localStorage back exactly (verified onnowtv-continue-watching-v1:p-a JSON identical, theme gold, library favorites/watchLater intact). Christmas chip is present as 2nd chip on /movies and loads 100 quality Christmas titles from /api/tmdb/by-genres/movie?genre_ids=-3. ProfileEdit synthetic list contains {id:-3,name:'Christmas'} at line 833. NOTE for future testing agents: the on-screen keyboard in ProfileLoad CodeStep uses stale-closure onClick — click each button with >=200ms spacing via `document.querySelector('[data-testid=profile-load-key-X]').click()` (JS native click after each wait). page.click via Playwright with 120ms intra-click delay drops chars. PIN pad on the PinGate step accepts real page.keyboard.type (numeric). Backup save page uses PIN typed via keyboard directly (no on-screen digits).
rca of the issue: N/A — all fixes verified. Christmas category correctly intersects TMDB keyword 207317 with Family|Comedy|Romance|Animation genres, yielding non-Harry-Potter results. ESSENTIAL_PREFIXES fix in profileBackup.js confirmed by non-zero counts in both save-summary and restore-preview (previously would have shown 0).
